title: The Teacher as Researcher: Action Research for Classroom Improvement
audience: K–12 Teachers; Subject Heads; Academic Directors; School Principals
aim: Transform educators from passive curriculum users into reflective, data-informed classroom designers who use short action-research cycles to improve learning outcomes.
performance_task: Submit an action-research canvas for one current classroom issue.
resources: Action Research Proposal Canvas; Baseline Data Mini-Tracker; Five-Day Intervention Log; Teacher Reflection Journal Page; Action Research Review Rubric

title: Multi-Tiered Support Systems: General, Intensified and Special Support
audience: K–12 Teachers; SENCOs; Counsellors; Academic Directors; School Leaders
aim: Equip educators to identify learning needs early and apply structured support through a flexible three-tier model adapted for different school contexts.
performance_task: Create a Tier 2 support plan for a learner with repeated difficulty.
resources: Three-Tier Adaptation Matrix; Support Decision Tree; Tier 2 Four-Week Intervention Log; Language Acquisition vs SEN Reflection Sheet; Parent Support Conversation Template

title: The Pedagogy of Play: Guided Play and Discovery Zones
audience: KG1 Teachers; KG2 Teachers; Early Childhood Leaders; Coordinators
aim: Help early childhood educators transform desk-based activities into structured, playful learning environments that develop language, numeracy, executive function and social skills.
performance_task: Convert one worksheet into a discovery-zone activity.
resources: Discovery Zone Planner; Open-Ended Prompt Bank; Executive Function Observation Checklist; Parent Explanation Handout: Why Play Is Learning; Play-Scaffold Conversion Blueprint

title: Formative Documentation and Joy of Learning
audience: Early Childhood Educators; Kindergarten Coordinators; Centre Directors
aim: Train educators to document young children’s learning through photos, child voice, teacher notes and growth conversations instead of comparative grading.
performance_task: Create a portfolio entry using a sample child observation.
resources: Narrative Documentation Sheet; Child Dictation Prompt Cards; Transversal Skill Tag Guide; Parent Growth Conversation Template; Digital Portfolio Workflow Checklist

title: Transversal Competences and the 45/15 Cognitive Rhythm
audience: Primary Teachers; Homeroom Teachers; Vice-Principals; Timetable Officers
aim: Help primary schools integrate transversal competences while structuring learning blocks to protect focus, movement and recovery.
performance_task: Submit one adapted morning schedule with competence mapping.
resources: 45/15 Timetable Planner; Transversal Competence Mapping Cards; Transition Routine Checklist; Indoor Movement Reset Menu; Leadership Schedule Audit Sheet

title: Student Autonomy and Formative Self-Assessment
audience: Primary Teachers; Grade Coordinators; Curriculum Designers
aim: Train teachers to build student independence through weekly learning contracts, choice pathways and descriptive self-assessment.
resources: Weekly Learning Contract Template; Student Self-Assessment Rubric Builder; Self-Correction Station Guide; Teacher Conference Script; Autonomy Readiness Checklist

title: Phenomenon-Based Learning Design
audience: Lower Secondary Teachers; Cross-Curricular Teams; Academic Directors
aim: Help teachers design interdisciplinary inquiry modules anchored in real-world phenomena and aligned to subject outcomes.
resources: PhenoBL Inquiry Canvas; Cross-Subject Standards Mapping Sheet; Student Research Question Cards; Group Accountability Rubric; Final Product Planning Template

title: Subject-Specific Didactics in High-Trust Environments
audience: Subject Specialists; Department Heads; Instructional Coaches
aim: Help subject teachers reduce passive lecture time and design high-trust discovery tasks that shift cognitive load to students.
resources: Teacher Talk-Time Tracker; Cognitive Load Pivot Framework; Guided Inquiry Question Bank; Student Evidence Capture Sheet; Peer Critique Protocol

title: Modular Upper Secondary Pathways and Individual Study Planning
audience: Upper Secondary Teachers; Advisors; Registrars; High School Principals
aim: Support upper secondary educators in designing modular study pathways, advising systems and student accountability structures.
resources: Modular Course Architecture Sheet; Individual Study Plan Template; Advisor Check-In Script; Prerequisite Mapping Chart; Student Milestone Dashboard

title: Annual Implementation, Quality Assurance and Administrative Rollout

aim: Enable school leaders to implement the Finnish Paradigm gradually through infrastructure review, staff training, curriculum activation and quality assurance.
resources: Annual Operational Timeline Template; School Readiness Audit; Quality Assurance Walkthrough Form; Teacher Talk-Time Tracker; Implementation Risk Register; Staff Training Rollout Planner
